{"score": 7.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 7.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 4.9, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:P/PR:L/UI:N/VC:N/VI:N/VA:N/SC:H/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-284", "name": "Improper Access Control"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-918", "name": "Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)"}], "description": "### Summary

OpenClaw's browser control SSRF checks blocked direct navigation to private or loopback URLs, but some Playwright `act` interactions could trigger navigation after the initial check. A later browser evaluation could then read from the page reached by that action-triggered navigation.

This issue is specific to browser control actions and private-network navigation policy. Browser evaluation remains an intentional trusted-operator feature when it is used on pages that policy allowed the browser to visit.

### Affected configurations

This affects deployments where browser control is enabled and an authenticated browser-control caller can interact with an attacker-controlled page that redirects or navigates the tab to a private-network target through a UI action.

### Impact

If the browser reached a private page through an unchecked action-triggered navigation, a caller with browser evaluation capability could read page content that direct navigation policy would have blocked.

The issue does not grant access to OpenClaw without authentication. It bypasses the private-network navigation guard for a specific browser action path.

### Patched Versions

The first stable patched version is `2026.5.18`.

### Mitigations

Upgrade to `openclaw@2026.5.18` or later. Before upgrading, restrict browser-control access to trusted operators and avoid using browser control on untrusted pages in environments with sensitive private web services.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00247, "percentile": 0.15691}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-2hfg-4fh4-qp7f", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-07-02T16:00:03Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2hfg-4fh4-qp7f", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-2hfg-4fh4-qp7f"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-53812"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-06-11T21:16:23Z", "published_at": "2026-07-02T16:00:03Z", "references": ["https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/security/advisories/GHSA-2hfg-4fh4-qp7f", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-53812", "https://www.vulncheck.com/advisories/openclaw-private-network-navigation-bypass-via-browser-act-interactions", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2hfg-4fh4-qp7f"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/openclaw/openclaw/security-advisories/GHSA-2hfg-4fh4-qp7f", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw", "summary": "OpenClaw's browser act interactions could bypass private-network navigation checks", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-07-02T16:00:03Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-2hfg-4fh4-qp7f", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "2026.5.18", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "openclaw"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 2026.5.18"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: OpenClaw's browser act interactions could bypass private-network navigation checks
